title: Nutritional and Metabolic Resilience
authors_full: Karen Sweazea, PhD, FAHA, FAPS, Associate Professor

summary.hook: This poster is a high-level overview of the Sweazea lab’s research themes under “Nutritional and Metabolic Resilience,” spanning comparative physiology, One Health food...
summary.summary_30s: This poster is a high-level overview of the Sweazea lab’s research themes under “Nutritional and Metabolic Resilience,” spanning comparative physiology, One Health food environments, and dietary interventions for metabolic disease.

talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: When you say “nutritional and metabolic resilience,” what core physiological outcomes define resilience in your lab’s work?

key_numbers: 1,995 common food items analyzed for GI/GL across NOVA levels; 1,946 food items analyzed for total antioxidant content.
Not stated on poster (study years given via citations: Sweazea et al. 2022; Basile et al. 2024; Healy et al. 2014).
Analysis of 1,995 food items found glycemic index and glycemic load do not differ across NOVA classification food...
Analysis of 1,946 food items supports minimally processed foods have higher total antioxidant content than processed or...
It also summarizes analyses of \~2,000 food items showing glycemic index/load do not differ across NOVA processing...
takeaways: Birds live longer and healthier lives than mammals of similar body mass despite having the highest blood sugar and lipid concentrations among vertebrates, motivating...
Analysis of 1,995 common food items found glycemic index and glycemic load do not differ across NOVA food processing levels.
Analysis of 1,946 food items supports that minimally processed foods have higher total antioxidant content than processed or ultra-processed foods.

general: What is the main takeaway you hope attendees remember from this poster?
